messages.text: The decorator tempering unit is not heating up. Maintenance is working on the issue.
We are already on the second reheat, and the temperatures haven't gone up; they are still holding at these levels.
Maintenance does not know what is wrong with the decorator tempering unit, we are waiting for Chris to get in.
Chris G reset the heating elements in the decorator tempering unit. The tempering unit is heating up now.
The mass cooling temperature hasn't risen to 30 degrees and we can't get a temper reading.Justin Garner
QA checked the caramel and approved it to start production
Maintenance identified that the temperature probe wasn't hooked up, that was why we could not get temper. Everything is working now and we are tempering on the mini tempering unit. Justin Garner
Enrober tempering
Is this part of a start up checklist?
No, it is not. We do not have anything for mini tempering unit set up.
Chocolate is too cold to even take a sample of. We are performing another reheat. Justin Garner
What was the settings of the machine?
That will be part of the RCA
Left side 31.5 right side 27.5
and it was cold?
Right now, a temperature reading is being taken; the right-side settings remain at 31.5, while the right side itself has been changed to 28.0.
What is the actual temp of the chocolate?

Good Morning,
Chiller was found OFF.
Machine is currently in melting.
The screen is in good condition.
Rice % in the range
The buffer tank temperature is below the acceptable range. Maintenance is here.
The first adjustment to the mixing water is performed at 6:12 AM; it drops to 84.3.
Lot and date is correct
The mixing auger stirrer (VDF) is not working. Maintenance is here.
We were very close to having another catastrophic belt failure on the imperial.
Demolding belt is off track maintenance is currently working on realignment of the belt
A second adjustment is made to the mixing water at 6:42 AM, lowering it to 84.1.
The belt was in this bad position on Sunday morning while un-tensioned for cleaning but was never centered before re-tensioning.
A third adjustment to the mixing water is performed at 7:10 AM; it drops to 83.9.
Maintenance continues working on the Imperial Belt.
Update Demolding belt is still having adjustments made by maintenance

Chad Stone Rich Brierley Robert Villarreal Preston Elmer The buffer tank was found at 40 on the gauge, with the tank set at 45. Is there a reason the set point continues to be lowered to 45–48? Each Monday, we raise the buffer tank set point to 53 to bring the chocolate temperature back within target range. I am not seeing step to lower it. I can add a step to PTL SD to leave temp at 53 on the tank.  Please let me know your thoughts
Please ensure everything is plugged in correctly.
1000% not plugged in correctly judging by that picture.
Demolding belt is good to go
Currently working on Temper issues.
Current temper is 1.0
I think we should remelt soon and set it at 82.5 right at the beginning. I think that recipe needs to be saved as at least 83. Robert Villarreal what do you think?
The problem right now is the buffer tank chocolate is too cold.
Teams are putting in molds at 10 mpm
Depositing at 7:52
A fourth adjustment is made to the mixing water at 7:51 AM; it drops to 83.7.
Tempermeter moved back into the correct location
The temperature is at 53; should it be at 50?
Robert Villarreal
The temper came out at 1.1. We stopped sending chocolate and are taking another temper.
The weight of the final piece is in the target, appearance, flavor and percentage of rice comply with quality standards
This is great for now, watch the chocolate temperature and lower to 51 when in the green
It is still on yellow.

Robert Villarreal Rich Brierley Line has stopped due to temper. Robert I remember last time during crunch we ran the temper unit using the milk recipe. If we continue to have temper issues would you like us to switch to milk recipe?
The temper is at 0.9. We already started depositing again

I don't believe you'll see a difference between the two. Every setting is the same besides the mixing water starting point. 
I will head over soon
The temper is at 1.2. We stopped depositing. Robert just arrived.
Turned up pre-treat temp from 115 to 119 to compensate for cold buffer temp
The temper is at 1.0. We already started depositing again
The temper is at 1.2. We stopped depositing.

Still here working out the kinkds. No adjustments made other than the Pre-Treat temperature but this one should come out better. 
Buffer tank temp is slowly rising but still in the yellow (cold) zone which is letting more chocolate crystals go by and affecting temper
Metal detector belt and c6 belt are running correctly
The temper is at 0.6. We already started depositing again
The packaging meets quality standards, in each display  up to 10 chocolate bars and in the master  case there are 18 displays
The temper is at 1.2. We stopped depositing.
The temper is at 0.9. We are depositing again.
Rich Brierley Chad Stone Robert Villarreal Grey Boscan . These three steps have been added to the SU checklist for the Inclusion hopper.

Buffer is sitting at 47 degrees right now. 
I will adjust water jacket down to 52 and continue monitoring. 
 
52 will be the ideal temp while in production 👌
Chiller
Little tank in between the swecos is not sending chocolate to the return or supply pipes, got full and overflowing.
Preston Elmer
Still tank is not suctioning chocolate. We have stopped the circulation of chocolate.
Nathan Ruiz can you come over to M4 as soon as you get here please
That's fine. As long as compressor 1 doesn't fault as well, the temperature fault is normal.

Correct. 

Itzel Chavez, is there a fault on the screen for tempering unit #1 or anywhere else why it isn’t sending chocolate?
Turn off the Theegarten Pactec pump please
The pump circled
It looks like the pipe that suctions the chocolate to buffer tank is froze up, it’s really cold, so as soon as we turn on tempering the tank starts overflowing.
This part is really cold.
Depositor 1 centerlines checklist and shadow board
This switch was off, I will add to the SU checklist to check that the switch is on.
Depositor 2 centerlines checklist
Waiting on temper.
Depositor 3 centerlines checklist
This is wrong again. But can anyone guess from this picture what the problem is? It's a sneaky one.
The hose on the left isn’t connected fully
Depositor 4 centerline temper checklist
First temper came out bad, waiting on the next one.
Ptls checklist and shadow board
2nd got in 0.8, made adjustments and waiting on the next one.
Slope 0.5, we are purging to start depositing.
Chiller.
We are depositing.
Depositor #4 temper
Itzel Chavez can you tell me how long it will be before the bars are at the back off plow?
Deposit layer 2 The weight is within the range.
Let me check.
Deposit layer 3 the weight is within the range of
Right now it’s at depositor#2 Preston Elmer at this speed I calculate another 25-35min to hit depositor #4.
Deposit layer 1
First Compressor 1 trip of the day, 10min ago

Preston Elmer when I was there on Saturday, compressor 1 was cycling on and off quite a bit as it hit set point (like you would expect during running). However the line was paused for some of that due to a mold jam so it maybe wasn't very representative of run scenario. When they are running like normal, does the compressor ever shut off?
Mold pass ATP✅
This option was off at the start of the shift, that is why we have a lot of molds not depositing because machine reads they are dirty.
Back off plow wipers were put in incorrectly on the south side. Tyson Fausett
They have been running at 10mpm, product is not to the final cooler yet so not at maximum load. But it has cycled off just now.
Deposit layer 4 the weight is within the range
This is a HUGE problem. The flat scraper would have hit the plow cleaner on the pointy edge and caused damage.
Speed has been increased to 13mpm.
Product hit demolding at 9:00am.
we will increase 1 mpm gradually every 30min until we are at 15mpm.

Is there spare scrapers I could look at and potentially laser engrave?
Preston
Yes, that would be awesome, even engrave the ones that are currently on the machine when we are down for cleaning.
I don't believe we have spares at the moment.

Itzel,
Please notify me of when these are available for me to look at and then engrave. It would be best if at that point someone could walk me through the setup process so I can be sure which side is which and other things like that. 
Preston,
Are we at risk of DT by not having spares? Not sure if these are easily breakable/bendable or if their only resistance would be the molds which shatter much easier than most machine parts
The weight of the chocolate is within the range.
the dimensions top chocolate and bottom are good
Down due to issues with Gondola.

Itzel Chavez

Please put the verbiage on a board of the different things we want to add to start up checklist. 

We can review them and then get them added to the start up checklist.
Will do!
Reference point issue again.
We are going to manually empty out Gondola, and perform a reference run.
The code lote and best by are correct
Still down due to this issue.
We have some missing tools
We received the machine with this configuration.
The line started at time

The pouch  have an adequate sealing ,





the letter is legible and the day and Lot is the same on the product in the bag on the final label
Change day done ✅

Good morning 


Incomplete shadow board—missing tool #13.
Please double check the area as it was there Saturday morning at the end of 3rd shift Molding Line 3 Pack, Jeffrey Barney
Chocolate adjustment report Alan Todd Alex Mendoza Brandon Lance
Nathan Topham please have team remove the sticky mat on Mezzanine it’s more of a safety risk to step on it than anything else.
QC has passed the line with ATP.  We are loading the bedding now
The line began operations at 7:15am. Justin,Nathan
Enrober CL.
Enrober tempering chart.

We have found out the truck for the peco pallets has broke down.  In the mean time we will stack on normal pallets and QC will put hold tags on those pallets till he have the correct ones. Justin and Katya
Sanitizer has been applied to the Enrober drain area - SoS.
CW MM3 MM4 MM5
We are having issues with the MD.  It is says fault: Drive to slow maintenance is looking into it now
Bed and caramel CL.
Line is back up and running
Correct pallets have arrived we have 5 pallets that we are having to restack onto correct pallets.  NOTHING will be on hold.  Justin and Katya
The correct Peco HT pallets specified in the specs have arrived, and the 5 pallets requiring rework are already being swapped out.
158 bags of scrap for 07: to 09: am
Weight checks - 9:00am.
BOM doesn't match are you wanting to run 10257 and 10226 for pretzel thins for WO 38308 Candon.
Nathan Topham happy Monday can someone update the BOM?
Nidia can you update the BOM please.
Paula Pena
Done Nathan Topham, Candon Watt

Justin Garner Curt Willis each line was ATP swabbed in 25-30 locations & APC sponges in 7-8 locations. We only have 1 ATP tester. 

Is there anyway you could uncover 1 line the 2 QA coming collect all ATPs on one line and then they can move to the other line? As soon as the ATP for one line is finished we can determine what needs recleaning, etc.  

Kathleen Gilbert Hervin Herrera

Justin Garner. Focus on s2 to start, QA and Charity Young will focus on ATP on that line to start tomorrow.

After S2 is good we can focus on S3 and the ATP on that line.  You can clean both lines, but S2 will be the focus to start up on Monday
Just a call out. All the belts in the jar area were left running over the weekend. Please make sure that everything is shut off at the end of the week. Everything was turned off. Joseph Scott

Curt Willis just a reminder I'm going to start disassembling the twist wrap cooler. Freon will be sucked out of it in 30 min and electrical will be disconnected after. 
 
Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth are here looking for people to clean it out.Chad Stone Jorel Gourdin Miriah Manzanares 
 
If I need to tarp the area I will.
Charity Young please have your team remove this from TW cooler.

post in S3 please
